---
spec-type: behavioral
concept: corpus-structure
---

# concept-index — the generated by-concept view of one corpus

The **concept-index** procedure: scan one spec corpus for every node's `concept:` frontmatter and
render a **by-concept view** — `concept → {its nodes across every folder}` — that re-unifies a
cross-cutting concern whose facets the capability folder tree scatters
([`../../design/spec-structure.md`](../../design/spec-structure.md), the concept axis). The view is
**pure derivation** from the `concept:` tags (the `../discovery/` no-drift rule): it is regenerated on
demand and lives in the root `spec.md` as a machine-owned block, never hand-maintained.

## Use Cases

**Subject** — deriving the by-concept index from node frontmatter and maintaining its block in the
root `spec.md`.
**Non-goals** — it never reads a node body (frontmatter only, like `../discovery/`), never edits
anything in `spec.md` outside its own delimited block, never writes lifecycle state, and never ranks
or judges (that is `../check-spec-structure/` and the spec gate).

| Trigger | Inputs | Outcome |
|---|---|---|
| **render the index** — a reader/agent needs every facet of a concern | a spec corpus dir | a by-concept table: each concept → its nodes, each annotated with its **facet kind** (rule / behavior / reference / e2e / index), derived purely from `concept:` + `spec-type` |
| **write the index** — refresh the block in `spec.md` | a corpus dir, write mode | the delimited generated block in the root `spec.md` is replaced with the freshly rendered table; **nothing else in `spec.md` changes** |
| **check drift** — a no-drift guard for CI | a corpus dir, check mode | succeeds iff the block already in `spec.md` equals the freshly rendered table; fails (non-zero) on any drift |
| **a node with no concept** | a node whose frontmatter declares no `concept` | the node is **omitted** from the index — not an error |
| **a node tagged with several concepts** | a node whose `concept` is a list | the node appears **under each** of its concepts |

Every scenario in [`concept-index.feature`](./concept-index.feature) maps to one of these entry
points or to the determinism/boundary rules that close this spec.

## The facet kind

Each node is annotated by **where its facet sits**, derived mechanically — so the index reads as
"concern X: its rule here, its behavior there":

- a node under `design/` → **rule**;
- a node under `acceptance/` → **e2e**;
- else `spec-type: reference` → **reference**;
- else `spec-type: behavioral` → **behavior**;
- else (a descriptive index) → **index**.

## Determinism and the write boundary

- **Pure derivation.** The table is a function of the `concept:` tags alone — **rendering twice yields
  byte-identical output**, and writing twice is a no-op. No body content reaches the output (frontmatter
  only).
- **Block-scoped write.** The write replaces only the content between the generated-block markers in
  `spec.md`; every other line — the lifecycle frontmatter, the prose, the capability map — is left
  untouched. When the markers are absent the block is inserted at the declared anchor, never scattered.
- **No registry.** The corpus is scanned live; the index consults no stored list of nodes or concepts —
  the `concept:` tags are the single source, so nothing can drift out of sync.
